key_insight: Midpoint-fused arches can be designed to be bistable, snapping between two force-free equilibrium shapes through nonlinear coupled deformation.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lightweight structural mechanism for deployable, switchable, and energy-storing systems inspired by umbrella-like rib inversion.
method: Built an analytical energy-minimization model for pinned-pinned midpoint-fused arches under a concentrated mid-span load.
Enforced kinematic compatibility at the fusion point to couple the deformation of individual arches.
Explored symmetric and asymmetric deformation pathways for two-arch and three-arch configurations.
evidence_signal: The analytical predictions were corroborated by nonlinear finite-element simulations and table-top experiments, with close agreement in both equilibrium states and transition responses.
watch_item: The bistable response depends strongly on boundary conditions, geometric symmetry, and pathway transitions, so fabrication tolerances and load placement may affect real-world switching behavior.
paper_title: Bistability of midpoint-fused arches with pinned-pinned boundary conditions
